tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and ketonuria, suggesting impaired renal function and possible acute or chronic kidney disease. Urine is cloudy, brown, and has low specific gravity, further supporting renal involvement. Blood glucose and HbA1c are low, indicating no current hyperglycemia. No evidence of urinary tract infection is present. Recommend clinical correlation for renal impairment and further nephrological evaluation.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
